project.name: markitdown
project.description: Lightweight Python utility for converting various files (PDF, Office documents, images, audio, HTML, EPubs, etc.) to Markdown for use with LLMs and text-analysis pipelines. Monorepo with four sub-packages: the core markitdown library, an MCP server, an OCR plugin, and a sample plugin.

nodes.name: _base_converter.py
nodes.summary: Defines the abstract base classes DocumentConverter and DocumentConverterResult that establish the conversion contract (accepts/convert) implemented by every format-specific converter in the project.

nodes.name: _exceptions.py
nodes.summary: Declares the exception hierarchy used throughout MarkItDown, including MissingDependencyException, UnsupportedFormatException, and FileConversionException for aggregating per-converter failure attempts.

nodes.name: _stream_info.py
nodes.summary: Defines the immutable StreamInfo dataclass that carries metadata (mimetype, extension, charset, filename, path, url) about a file stream between the dispatcher and converters.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a frozen, kw_only dataclass with a copy_and_update helper that merges non-None fields from other StreamInfo instances, mimicking immutable update patterns.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel that imports and re-exports every concrete converter class (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, HTML, audio, image, YouTube, ZIP, etc.) for convenient access via the converters package.

nodes.name: _audio_converter.py
nodes.summary: Converts audio files (wav, mp3, m4a) to Markdown by extracting EXIF/ID3 metadata via exiftool and optionally transcribing speech content to text.

nodes.name: _csv_converter.py
nodes.summary: Converts CSV files to Markdown tables using pandas, with charset detection and configurable row/column limits.

nodes.name: _cu_converter.py
nodes.summary: Implements the ContentUnderstandingConverter that submits documents, images, audio, and video to Azure AI Content Understanding analyzers and converts the structured analysis results into Markdown.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains an extensive MIME-type/file-type detection pipeline (multiple helper functions resolving extensions, MIME types, and analyzer modalities) layered on top of polling-based Azure REST API calls.

nodes.name: _doc_intel_converter.py
nodes.summary: Implements the DocumentIntelligenceConverter that sends documents (PDF, Office formats, images) to Azure Document Intelligence for layout/OCR analysis and renders the result as Markdown.

nodes.name: DocumentConverterResult
nodes.summary: Wraps the output of a document conversion, holding the resulting Markdown text and an optional title, with a soft-deprecated text_content alias and __str__ returning the Markdown.

nodes.name: DocumentConverter
nodes.summary: Abstract superclass for all document converters, defining the accepts() and convert() contract that subclasses must implement to detect and transform documents into Markdown.

nodes.name: MarkItDownException
nodes.summary: Root exception class for all errors raised explicitly by MarkItDown.

nodes.name: MissingDependencyException
nodes.summary: Raised when a converter requires an optional dependency that is not installed, guiding the user toward the correct pip extras to install.

nodes.name: UnsupportedFormatException
nodes.summary: Raised when no available converter declares that it can handle the given file format.

nodes.name: FailedConversionAttempt
nodes.summary: Lightweight value object that records a single converter's failed attempt, pairing the converter instance with its captured exception info for later reporting.

nodes.name: FileConversionException
nodes.summary: Aggregate exception raised when every applicable converter fails, formatting a combined traceback message from the list of FailedConversionAttempt records.

nodes.name: StreamInfo
nodes.summary: Immutable, frozen dataclass carrying optional metadata (mimetype, extension, charset, filename, local_path, url) describing a file stream, with a copy_and_update method for non-destructive merging of fields.

nodes.name: AudioConverter
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter implementation that accepts wav/mp3/m4a streams, extracts metadata fields via exiftool, and appends a speech transcript when transcription succeeds.

nodes.name: CsvConverter
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter implementation that detects CSV streams by mimetype/extension and renders them as a Markdown table via pandas.read_csv and to_markdown.

nodes.name: ContentUnderstandingFileType
nodes.summary: Enumeration-like definition of file type categories (document, audio, video, image) and their associated MIME types/extensions recognized by the Azure Content Understanding service.

nodes.name: ContentUnderstandingConverter
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter implementation that submits a file stream to an Azure AI Content Understanding analyzer, polls for completion, and renders the structured analysis result as Markdown.

nodes.name: _get_modality
nodes.summary: Maps a ContentUnderstandingFileType to the Azure analyzer 'modality' string used when configuring or selecting an analyzer.

nodes.name: _detect_file_type
nodes.summary: Determines the ContentUnderstandingFileType of a stream by matching its mimetype and extension against the supported type definitions.

nodes.name: _clean_mime_type
nodes.summary: Normalizes a raw MIME type string by stripping parameters such as charset for consistent comparisons.

nodes.name: _canonical_mime_type
nodes.summary: Resolves a MIME type to its canonical form, used to bridge differences between guessed and declared MIME types.

nodes.name: _content_type_for
nodes.summary: Derives the best-guess Content-Type header value for a stream based on its StreamInfo metadata and filename/extension fallbacks.

nodes.name: _detect_file_type_from_mime
nodes.summary: Resolves a ContentUnderstandingFileType purely from a MIME type string, used as a fallback when extension-based detection is inconclusive.

nodes.name: _resolve_analyzer_modality
nodes.summary: Determines which analyzer modality should be used for a given stream by combining file-type detection with any explicitly configured analyzer settings.

nodes.name: _is_analyzer_compatible
nodes.summary: Checks whether a configured Azure analyzer's modality is compatible with the detected modality of the input stream before attempting conversion.

nodes.name: DocumentIntelligenceFileType
nodes.summary: Defines the set of file type categories and their MIME-type/extension mappings supported by the Azure Document Intelligence service.

nodes.name: DocumentIntelligenceConverter
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter implementation that sends a document to an Azure Document Intelligence endpoint for layout/OCR analysis and renders the returned structure (pages, tables, headers) as Markdown.

nodes.name: _get_mime_type_prefixes
nodes.summary: Builds the list of MIME-type prefixes that DocumentIntelligenceConverter recognizes, used by accepts() to perform fast format checks.

nodes.name: _get_file_extensions
nodes.summary: Builds the list of file extensions that DocumentIntelligenceConverter recognizes, complementing the MIME-type based detection in accepts().

nodes.name: _plain_text_converter.py
nodes.summary: Converts plain-text and JSON-like text files to Markdown by decoding the byte stream using the detected or guessed charset.

nodes.name: _pptx_converter.py
nodes.summary: Converts PowerPoint (.pptx) presentations to Markdown using python-pptx, rendering slide titles, text, tables, charts, and images (with optional LLM captioning) slide by slide.

nodes.name: _transcribe_audio.py
nodes.summary: Provides the transcribe_audio helper that converts audio streams to text via SpeechRecognition/Google speech APIs, transcoding mp3/mp4 to wav with pydub when needed.
nodes.languageNotes: Defers optional-dependency import errors until the function is actually called, surfacing them as a MissingDependencyException with the original traceback preserved.

nodes.name: _xlsx_converter.py
nodes.summary: Converts Excel spreadsheets to Markdown, with XlsxConverter handling modern .xlsx files via openpyxl/pandas and XlsConverter handling legacy .xls files via xlrd, both rendering each sheet as an HTML table converted through the HTML converter.

nodes.name: _youtube_converter.py
nodes.summary: Converts YouTube video URLs to Markdown by scraping page metadata (title, description, view/keyword data) and fetching available transcripts/captions via the YouTube Transcript API.

nodes.name: _zip_converter.py
nodes.summary: Converts ZIP archives to Markdown by extracting each contained file to a temporary directory and recursively converting and concatenating the results via the parent MarkItDown instance.

nodes.name: PlainTextConverter
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter implementation that decodes plain-text or text-like (e.g., JSON) byte streams using the detected charset and returns the content unchanged as Markdown.

nodes.name: PptxConverter
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter implementation that walks PowerPoint slides via python-pptx and renders titles, body text, tables, charts, and pictures (with optional LLM captions) as Markdown.

nodes.name: transcribe_audio
nodes.summary: Transcribes an audio stream to text using SpeechRecognition's Google speech API, transcoding mp3/mp4 input to wav via pydub when required and raising MissingDependencyException if optional libraries are absent.

nodes.name: XlsxConverter
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter implementation that converts modern .xlsx workbooks to Markdown by rendering each worksheet as an HTML table via pandas/openpyxl and delegating to the HTML converter.

nodes.name: XlsConverter
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter implementation that converts legacy .xls workbooks to Markdown by reading sheets via xlrd/pandas and rendering them as HTML tables through the HTML converter.

nodes.name: YouTubeConverter
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter implementation that detects YouTube watch URLs, scrapes page metadata (title, description, stats) from embedded JSON, and fetches video transcripts via the YouTube Transcript API with retry logic.

nodes.name: ZipConverter
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter implementation that extracts ZIP archive members to a temporary directory and recursively converts each file via the parent MarkItDown instance, concatenating results into a single Markdown document.

nodes.name: _epub_converter.py
nodes.summary: Converts EPUB e-book files to Markdown by parsing the archive's metadata and chapter HTML content and delegating HTML-to-Markdown rendering to the HTML converter.

nodes.name: _exiftool.py
nodes.summary: Provides a helper that shells out to the exiftool binary to extract JSON metadata from a file stream, including a version check guarding against CVE-2021-22204.

nodes.name: _html_converter.py
nodes.summary: Converts HTML documents and strings to Markdown using BeautifulSoup for parsing and a custom Markdownify subclass for rendering, stripping scripts/styles and extracting the page title.

nodes.name: _image_converter.py
nodes.summary: Converts image files to Markdown by extracting EXIF metadata via exiftool and optionally generating an LLM-based image caption/description.

nodes.name: _ipynb_converter.py
nodes.summary: Converts Jupyter Notebook (.ipynb) files to Markdown by walking notebook cells and rendering markdown, code, and outputs (including images) in document order.

nodes.name: _llm_caption.py
nodes.summary: Provides the llm_caption helper that base64-encodes an image stream and calls an OpenAI-compatible chat completion client to produce a descriptive caption.

nodes.name: _outlook_msg_converter.py
nodes.summary: Converts Outlook .msg email files to Markdown by parsing the OLE compound file structure to extract sender, recipient, subject, and message body.

nodes.name: _pdf_converter.py
nodes.summary: Converts PDF documents to Markdown using pdfminer, including specialized logic to reconstruct numbered lists, render extracted word-level layout into Markdown tables, and detect/extract form fields and tabular regions.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of geometric/positional heuristics (bounding boxes, gap thresholds) to reconstruct tables and form layouts from raw word coordinates extracted by pdfminer.

nodes.name: EpubConverter
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter implementation that opens an EPUB archive, extracts metadata (title, author) and chapter HTML, and converts the combined content to Markdown via the HTML converter.

nodes.name: exiftool_metadata
nodes.summary: Runs the exiftool binary as a subprocess against a file stream, validates its version against CVE-2021-22204, and returns the extracted metadata as parsed JSON.

nodes.name: HtmlConverter
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter implementation that parses HTML with BeautifulSoup, removes scripts/styles, extracts the document title, and renders the body as Markdown using a custom Markdownify subclass.

nodes.name: ImageConverter
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter implementation that extracts EXIF metadata from image streams via exiftool and optionally generates an LLM-based caption/description of the image content.

nodes.name: IpynbConverter
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter implementation that parses Jupyter Notebook JSON structure and renders markdown cells, code cells, and their outputs (text, images) as ordered Markdown content.

nodes.name: llm_caption
nodes.summary: Encodes an image stream as a base64 data URI and sends it with a prompt to an OpenAI-compatible chat completion client to generate a descriptive caption.

nodes.name: OutlookMsgConverter
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter implementation that parses Outlook .msg OLE compound files via olefile to extract sender, recipient, subject, and body, rendering them as a formatted Markdown email summary.

nodes.name: PdfConverter
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter implementation that extracts text, tables, and form content from PDF documents using pdfminer and the module's layout-analysis helper functions, producing structured Markdown output.

nodes.name: _merge_partial_numbering_lines
nodes.summary: Post-processes extracted PDF text lines to merge numbering markers (e.g., '1.', 'a)') that were split from their following content during raw extraction.

nodes.name: _to_markdown_table
nodes.summary: Renders a 2D grid of extracted cell strings into a properly formatted Markdown table with header separator rows.

nodes.name: _extract_form_content_from_words
nodes.summary: Analyzes word-level layout coordinates from a PDF page to detect and reconstruct form-like field/value structures using positional heuristics.

nodes.name: _extract_tables_from_words
nodes.summary: Groups word-level layout coordinates from a PDF page into rows and columns to detect tabular regions and reconstruct them as Markdown tables.

nodes.name: __about__.py
nodes.summary: Defines the package version string for the markitdown-ocr plugin distribution.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package entry point that re-exports the plugin interface, version, OCR service classes, and all OCR-enhanced converters as the public API of markitdown-ocr.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel using relative imports and an explicit __all__ list to define the public surface.

nodes.name: _docx_converter_with_ocr.py
nodes.summary: Implements an OCR-enhanced DOCX converter that extracts embedded images from Word documents, runs them through an LLM vision OCR service, and injects the recognized text as Markdown placeholders alongside the converted HTML-to-Markdown content.
nodes.languageNotes: Subclasses HtmlConverter and overrides convert() to pre-process the document twice: once to extract+OCR images, and once to render final Markdown with placeholder substitution via regex.

nodes.name: DocxConverterWithOCR
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter subclass that converts DOCX files to Markdown while replacing embedded images with OCR-extracted text via placeholder injection and regex substitution.

nodes.name: _ocr_service.py
nodes.summary: Provides the OCR service layer used by all format converters: an OCRResult dataclass for structured results and an LLMVisionOCRService that sends base64-encoded images to an OpenAI-compatible vision model to extract text.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses @dataclass for the result type and base64 data-URI encoding to pass images to a chat-completions-style vision API.

nodes.name: OCRResult
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing the outcome of an OCR extraction attempt, including extracted text, confidence, backend identifier, and optional error message.

nodes.name: LLMVisionOCRService
nodes.summary: OCR service backed by an LLM vision model (OpenAI-compatible client) that base64-encodes an image stream, sends it with a text-extraction prompt to a chat completions endpoint, and returns the recognized text wrapped in an OCRResult.

nodes.name: _pdf_converter_with_ocr.py
nodes.summary: Implements an OCR-enhanced PDF converter that extracts text via pdfminer/pdfplumber, detects and OCRs embedded images and chart/diagram regions, and falls back to full-page image rendering and OCR (via PyMuPDF/fitz) when the PDF is scanned or text extraction yields too little content.
nodes.languageNotes: Combines multiple PDF libraries (pdfplumber, pdfminer.six, PyMuPDF/fitz, Pillow) and interleaves extracted text blocks with OCR results sorted by vertical position to reconstruct reading order.

nodes.name: _extract_images_from_page
nodes.summary: Module-level helper that inspects a pdfplumber page's embedded objects, decodes raster images (with Pillow fallback to a cropped page render), and returns a list of image metadata dictionaries with bounding boxes and byte streams.

nodes.name: PdfConverterWithOCR
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter subclass that converts PDF files to Markdown by combining text extraction with OCR of embedded images and, when needed, full-page OCR of scanned documents, merging results in vertical reading order.

nodes.name: _plugin.py
nodes.summary: Plugin registration module that wires up an LLMVisionOCRService from supplied LLM client/model kwargs and registers all four OCR-enhanced converters (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX) with MarkItDown at a higher priority than the built-in converters, effectively replacing them.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements MarkItDown's plugin interface contract via __plugin_interface_version__ and a register_converters(markitdown, **kwargs) hook function.

nodes.name: register_converters
nodes.summary: Plugin hook that builds an optional LLMVisionOCRService from llm_client/llm_model/llm_prompt kwargs and registers OCR-enhanced PDF, DOCX, PPTX, and XLSX converters with the given MarkItDown instance at priority -1.0 so they run before built-in converters.

nodes.name: _pptx_converter_with_ocr.py
nodes.summary: Implements an OCR-enhanced PowerPoint converter that walks slide shapes, OCRs/captions embedded pictures via the LLM vision service, and converts tables and charts to Markdown, assembling the result into structured slide-by-slide Markdown output.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses nested helper function get_shape_content (including recursive handling for grouped shapes) and converts native PPTX tables/charts to HTML before delegating to HtmlConverter for Markdown rendering.

nodes.name: PptxConverterWithOCR
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter subclass that converts PPTX presentations to Markdown, OCRing or captioning embedded images, rendering tables/charts to Markdown via an internal HtmlConverter, and preserving slide order and shape layout.

nodes.name: _xlsx_converter_with_ocr.py
nodes.summary: Implements an OCR-enhanced Excel converter that renders spreadsheet data to Markdown tables via pandas/HtmlConverter and, when an OCR service is configured, additionally extracts and OCRs images embedded in worksheets, reporting their cell locations.
nodes.languageNotes: Branches between a fast pandas-based standard path (_convert_standard) and an OpenPyXL-based OCR path (_convert_with_ocr) depending on whether an OCR service is available.

nodes.name: XlsxConverterWithOCR
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter subclass that converts XLSX workbooks to Markdown tables and, when OCR is enabled, extracts and recognizes text in embedded worksheet images, mapping results back to their originating cells.

nodes.name: __about__.py
nodes.summary: Tiny module holding the package version string (`__version__`), used by the public API and the CLI's `--version` flag.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel that re-exports the public API surface of markitdown — `MarkItDown`, converter base classes, `StreamInfo`, exception types, priority constants, and the package version — defining `__all__`.
nodes.languageNotes: Classic Python package `__init__.py` barrel pattern: imports from sibling private modules (prefixed with `_`) and re-exports them via an explicit `__all__` list to define the public API.

nodes.name: __main__.py
nodes.summary: Command-line interface entry point for markitdown: parses CLI arguments (input file, output file, format hints, plugin and Document Intelligence/Content Understanding options), invokes `MarkItDown` to perform the conversion, and writes the resulting Markdown to stdout or a file.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `argparse` with a custom `RawDescriptionHelpFormatter` and `textwrap.dedent` to render multi-line usage text, and `importlib.metadata.entry_points` to list installed plugins.

nodes.name: _markitdown.py
nodes.summary: Defines the core `MarkItDown` orchestrator class that registers built-in and plugin converters, detects file type via MIME/extension/magika heuristics (`StreamInfo` guesses), and dispatches local files, streams, URLs, URIs, and HTTP responses to the appropriate `DocumentConverter` for Markdown conversion.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of priority-ordered converter registration (`PRIORITY_SPECIFIC_FILE_FORMAT` vs `PRIORITY_GENERIC_FILE_FORMAT`), lazy plugin loading via `importlib.metadata.entry_points`, and layered `StreamInfo` guesses combining extension, MIME type, and content-sniffing (magika, charset_normalizer).

nodes.name: _uri_utils.py
nodes.summary: Small URI helper module providing `file_uri_to_path` to convert `file://` URIs to filesystem paths and `parse_data_uri` to decode `data:` URIs into MIME type, charset, and raw bytes.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry function that builds the argparse parser, validates extension/MIME/charset hints, lists installed plugins on request, constructs a `MarkItDown` instance with the requested options (plugins, Document Intelligence, Content Understanding), runs the conversion (`convert_stream` or `convert`), and hands the result to `_handle_output`.

nodes.name: _handle_output
nodes.summary: Writes the conversion result's Markdown either to a specified output file or to stdout, encoding/decoding to handle platform charset quirks gracefully.

nodes.name: _load_plugins
nodes.summary: Lazily discovers and loads installed `markitdown.plugin` entry points exactly once, caching the result in a module-level `_plugins` list and warning (without failing) when an individual plugin fails to load.

nodes.name: ConverterRegistration
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass pairing a registered `DocumentConverter` instance with its dispatch priority, used internally to maintain the ordered converter registry.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `@dataclass(kw_only=True, frozen=True)` for an immutable, keyword-only value object.

nodes.name: MarkItDown
nodes.summary: The central facade/orchestrator class of the library: registers built-in and plugin `DocumentConverter`s in priority order, detects content type via extension/MIME/magika/charset heuristics, and exposes `convert`, `convert_local`, `convert_stream`, `convert_url`, `convert_uri`, and `convert_response` to transform local files, byte streams, URLs/URIs, and HTTP responses into Markdown via `DocumentConverterResult`.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements a priority-ordered converter dispatch pipeline (`_convert`) that tries each registered converter's `accepts()` check and collects `FailedConversionAttempt`s before raising `FileConversionException`/`UnsupportedFormatException`.

nodes.name: file_uri_to_path
nodes.summary: Converts a `file://` URI into an absolute local filesystem path using `urlparse` and `url2pathname`, raising `ValueError` for non-file schemes.

nodes.name: parse_data_uri
nodes.summary: Parses a `data:` URI into its MIME type, optional charset, and decoded payload bytes, supporting both base64-encoded and percent-encoded data segments.

nodes.name: latex_dict.py
nodes.summary: Static lookup-table module mapping Unicode/OMML math symbols, accents, brackets, functions, and layout templates to their LaTeX string equivalents, adapted from the dwml project. Provides the dictionaries (CHR, CHR_BO, T, FUNC, D, RAD, M, etc.) and template strings consumed by the OMML-to-LaTeX converter.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure-data module: no functions or classes, only module-level dict/tuple/string constants used as translation tables and format-string templates (e.g. "\\sqrt[{deg}]{{{text}}}").

nodes.name: omml.py
nodes.summary: Parses Office Math Markup Language (OMML) XML elements and converts them into LaTeX strings, dispatching on element tag names via the oMath2Latex visitor class and the symbol tables from latex_dict. Also exposes load/load_string convenience functions for parsing whole XML documents or strings into LaTeX equations.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements a tag-dispatch 'visitor' pattern via Tag2Method.call_method, which derives method names like do_<tag> from XML element tag names and reflectively invokes them — a common technique for walking heterogeneous XML trees without long if/elif chains.

nodes.name: pre_process.py
nodes.summary: Pre-processes a raw DOCX (zip/XML) byte stream before HTML conversion by finding OMML math equation elements (oMath/oMathPara) in the document XML and replacing them with their LaTeX-equivalent text spans, then re-zips the modified document for downstream conversion.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses BeautifulSoup to locate and replace XML tags in-place, and the zipfile/io modules to unpack a DOCX (which is a ZIP archive of XML parts), patch document.xml, and repackage it into a new in-memory archive.

nodes.name: _docx_converter.py
nodes.summary: Defines DocxConverter, a DocumentConverter (via HtmlConverter) that converts .docx files to Markdown by pre-processing math equations, converting the document to HTML with the optional 'mammoth' dependency, and delegating final Markdown rendering to an internal HtmlConverter instance.
nodes.languageNotes: Performs a lazy/optional-dependency check pattern: it attempts `import mammoth` at module load time, stores any ImportError's exc_info, and re-raises a MissingDependencyException with the original traceback only when convert() is actually invoked — avoiding a hard crash when the optional 'docx' extra isn't installed.

nodes.name: load
nodes.summary: Parses an XML stream with ElementTree, finds all oMath elements, and converts each to a LaTeX string via oMath2Latex.

nodes.name: load_string
nodes.summary: Parses an XML string with ElementTree, finds all oMath elements, and converts each to a LaTeX string via oMath2Latex.

nodes.name: escape_latex
nodes.summary: Escapes LaTeX special characters (braces, underscores, ampersands, etc.) in a string by prefixing them with a backslash, returning a safe LaTeX-renderable string.

nodes.name: get_val
nodes.summary: Looks up a value for a key in a dictionary-like 'store', falling back to a default when absent — a small helper used throughout the OMML element handlers to read formatting properties.

nodes.name: Tag2Method
nodes.summary: Mixin/base class implementing a tag-name-to-method dispatch mechanism: derives a 'do_<tag>' method name from an XML element's tag and invokes it (or process_unknow as fallback), enabling subclasses to handle arbitrary OMML element types via naming convention.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Python's getattr-based reflection (getmethod) to map XML tag strings to same-named instance methods — a lightweight alternative to large dispatch dictionaries or if/elif chains.

nodes.name: Pr
nodes.summary: Represents OMML 'properties' (formatting/control) elements (e.g. line breaks, common attributes), processing their children into an internal dict and exposing values via dynamic attribute access (__getattr__).

nodes.name: oMath2Latex
nodes.summary: Core converter class that walks an OMML <oMath> element tree and renders it into a LaTeX string, with one do_<tag> handler per OMML construct (fractions, radicals, accents, sub/superscripts, n-ary operators, matrices, limits, function names, groups, runs of text, etc.).
nodes.languageNotes: Each OMML element type (acc, bar, d, sub, sup, f, func, rad, eqArr, limLow, limUpp, m, mr, nary, r, ...) gets its own do_<tag> method that composes LaTeX template strings from latex_dict with recursively-processed children — a textbook recursive-descent tree-to-string transformer.

nodes.name: _convert_omath_to_latex
nodes.summary: Wraps a raw OMML tag fragment in a math-root XML template, parses it with ElementTree, locates the inner oMath element, and converts it to a LaTeX string via oMath2Latex.

nodes.name: _get_omath_tag_replacement
nodes.summary: Builds a replacement BeautifulSoup tag (span/div) wrapping the LaTeX-rendered form of an OMML equation, choosing inline vs. block-level wrapping markup based on the 'block' flag.

nodes.name: _replace_equations
nodes.summary: Finds nested OMML equation tags (oMath/oMathPara) within a parsed XML tag and replaces each one in-place with its LaTeX-rendered HTML span/div equivalent, raising ValueError on unsupported nesting.

nodes.name: _pre_process_math
nodes.summary: Parses a document.xml byte string with BeautifulSoup, finds top-level oMathPara and oMath elements, replaces each with LaTeX-rendered markup via _replace_equations, and re-serializes the modified XML back to bytes.

nodes.name: pre_process_docx
nodes.summary: Top-level entry point that unpacks a .docx (ZIP) byte stream, rewrites document.xml (and headers/footnotes if present) to inline LaTeX renderings of math equations via _pre_process_math, repackages everything into a new in-memory ZIP, and returns it ready for HTML conversion.
nodes.languageNotes: Manipulates a DOCX as a ZIP archive in-memory using zipfile and io.BytesIO, copying all archive members while patching only the XML parts that can contain math (document.xml, header*.xml, footer*.xml, footnotes.xml, endnotes.xml).

nodes.name: DocxConverter
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter implementation (subclassing HtmlConverter) that recognizes .docx files by MIME type or extension, pre-processes math equations, converts the document to HTML using the optional 'mammoth' library, and renders the final Markdown via an internal HtmlConverter instance.
nodes.languageNotes: Demonstrates converter composition/delegation: rather than reimplementing HTML-to-Markdown rendering, DocxConverter wraps a fresh HtmlConverter instance and forwards mammoth's HTML output to it, while inheriting DocumentConverter's interface from HtmlConverter.

nodes.name: _bing_serp_converter.py
nodes.summary: Defines BingSerpConverter, a DocumentConverter that recognizes Bing search-results pages by URL pattern, strips UI cruft, decodes base64-obfuscated redirect links, and renders the organic search results to Markdown via _CustomMarkdownify.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses regex URL matching plus BeautifulSoup DOM traversal, and decodes Bing's base64url-encoded redirect parameter (RFC 4648) to recover original result URLs.

nodes.name: BingSerpConverter
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter subclass that detects Bing SERP HTML by URL pattern and MIME/extension, then extracts and Markdown-renders only the organic search results, decoding redirect URLs and cleaning up Bing-specific markup artifacts.

nodes.name: _markdownify.py
nodes.summary: Provides _CustomMarkdownify, a subclass of the markdownify library's MarkdownConverter that customizes heading style, sanitizes hyperlinks (removing javascript: links and escaping URIs), truncates large data-URI images, and converts checkbox inputs to Markdown task syntax. Used as the shared HTML-to-Markdown rendering engine across multiple converters.
nodes.languageNotes: Overrides specific markdownify converter methods (convert_hn, convert_a, convert_img, convert_input, convert_soup) to layer project-specific sanitization on top of the third-party library's default HTML element handling.

nodes.name: _CustomMarkdownify
nodes.summary: Customized markdownify.MarkdownConverter that forces ATX heading style, strips javascript: links, escapes/normalizes URIs in anchors, truncates data: URI images unless explicitly kept, and renders HTML checkboxes as Markdown '[x]'/'[ ]' syntax.

nodes.name: _rss_converter.py
nodes.summary: Defines RssConverter, a DocumentConverter that detects and parses RSS and Atom feeds (via MIME type, extension, or sniffing the XML root element), then converts feed channel/entry metadata and HTML-formatted content into structured Markdown using _CustomMarkdownify for embedded HTML fragments.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses defusedxml's minidom for safe XML parsing, distinguishes RSS vs. Atom by inspecting root tag names (<rss> vs. <feed>/<entry>), and falls back to BeautifulSoup + _CustomMarkdownify to render HTML-styled feed item content.

nodes.name: RssConverter
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter that identifies RSS/Atom XML feeds (by extension, MIME type, or content sniffing), parses channel/feed and item/entry elements with minidom, and emits Markdown with titles, publish dates, and HTML-rendered descriptions or content blocks.

nodes.name: _wikipedia_converter.py
nodes.summary: Defines WikipediaConverter, a DocumentConverter that recognizes Wikipedia article URLs, strips scripts/styles and chrome from the page, isolates the main article body and title, and renders the cleaned content to Markdown via _CustomMarkdownify.
nodes.languageNotes: Targets Wikipedia's known DOM structure (#mw-content-text body, .mw-page-title-main title) to extract only the article content, falling back to converting the whole page if the expected elements are absent.

nodes.name: WikipediaConverter
nodes.summary: DocumentConverter subclass that detects Wikipedia article pages via URL regex and MIME/extension checks, then extracts the main content div and page title from the DOM and converts them to Markdown, ignoring navigation chrome and scripts/styles.

nodes.name: __about__.py
nodes.summary: Defines the package version string for the markitdown-sample-plugin reference plugin, following the standard Hatch/PyPI `__about__.py` convention.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package entry point for the sample plugin that re-exports the plugin interface version, the converter registration hook, and the RtfConverter class for external consumption.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel file (`__init__.py`) that aggregates and re-exports symbols via `__all__`, the conventional way plugin authors expose a stable public API.

nodes.name: _plugin.py
nodes.summary: Reference implementation of a markitdown plugin: declares the plugin interface version, provides a `register_converters` hook, and implements `RtfConverter`, a minimal DocumentConverter that turns RTF files into Markdown using the `striprtf` library.
nodes.languageNotes: Demonstrates the markitdown plugin contract: a module-level `__plugin_interface_version__`, a `register_converters(markitdown, **kwargs)` hook for registration via entry points, and a `DocumentConverter` subclass implementing `accepts`/`convert`.

nodes.name: register_converters
nodes.summary: Plugin registration hook called when a MarkItDown instance is constructed; instantiates and attaches an RtfConverter to the given MarkItDown instance.

nodes.name: RtfConverter
nodes.summary: Minimal DocumentConverter implementation that detects RTF files by MIME type or extension and converts their contents to Markdown text via the `striprtf.rtf_to_text` helper.
nodes.languageNotes: Subclasses the core `DocumentConverter` base class and implements its two-method contract (`accepts` for format detection, `convert` for transformation), the canonical pattern third-party plugins follow.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Root Docker build for the MarkItDown CLI image: based on python:3.13-slim-bullseye, installs ffmpeg and exiftool runtime dependencies, optionally installs git, copies the repo into /app, pip-installs the core `markitdown[all]` package plus the sample plugin, and runs as a non-root user with `markitdown` as the entrypoint.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses build ARGs (INSTALL_GIT, USERID, GROUPID) to make the image configurable for optional git support and non-root execution, and installs both the core package and the sample plugin via pip in one layer.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Docker build context filter that excludes everything except the `packages/` directory, keeping the image build context minimal and avoiding leakage of unrelated repo files into the build.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Docker build for the `markitdown-mcp` package — an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that exposes MarkItDown's file-to-Markdown conversion as a tool for LLM agents. Based on python:3.13-slim-bullseye, it installs ffmpeg and exiftool, enables plugins via `MARKITDOWN_ENABLE_PLUGINS=True`, copies the package source, pip-installs it, runs as a non-root user from a `/workdir` working directory, and launches the `markitdown-mcp` server as the entrypoint.
nodes.languageNotes: Sets MARKITDOWN_ENABLE_PLUGINS=True at the environment level so the MCP server exposes third-party MarkItDown plugins to connected LLM clients out of the box, and uses configurable USERID/GROUPID build ARGs to run as a non-root user.

nodes.name: pre-commit.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that runs on every pull request: it checks out the code, sets up Python, installs `pre-commit`, registers the project's hooks, and then runs `pre-commit run --all-files` to enforce linting/formatting (e.g., Black) across the entire codebase.

nodes.name: tests.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that runs on every pull request: it checks out the code, sets up multiple Python versions (3.10, 3.11, 3.12), installs Hatch via pipx, and runs `hatch test` inside `packages/markitdown` to execute the core package's test suite across all supported interpreters.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a multi-version Python matrix (3.10-3.12) to validate compatibility with the project's minimum supported Python version stated in the README (3.10+).

nodes.name: .pre-commit-config.yaml
nodes.summary: Pre-commit hook configuration that registers the `psf/black` repo (pinned to rev 23.7.0) so the `black` formatter hook runs automatically on commits and in CI to enforce consistent Python code style.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Primary project documentation for MarkItDown, a lightweight Python utility that converts PDFs, Office documents, images, audio, HTML, ZIP archives, YouTube URLs, EPubs and more into LLM-friendly Markdown. Covers installation (pip/source/Docker), CLI and Python API usage, optional dependency groups, the plugin system (including the `markitdown-ocr` plugin), Azure Document Intelligence and Azure Content Understanding integrations, contribution guidelines, running tests via Hatch, security considerations around untrusted input, and trademark notices.
nodes.languageNotes: Structured as a long-form Markdown guide with 20 headings spanning installation, usage, plugin ecosystem, and Azure cloud-conversion integrations, including embedded Python and shell code samples that double as living usage documentation.

nodes.name: SECURITY.md
nodes.summary: Standard Microsoft Open Source security policy describing how to responsibly report vulnerabilities found in the repository (via MSRC rather than public GitHub issues), preferred languages, and the Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure policy.

nodes.name: SUPPORT.md

nodes.name: README.md

nodes.name: ThirdPartyNotices.md

nodes.languageNotes: Uses Hatchling's dynamic versioning (reads version from `__about__.py`) and `[project.optional-dependencies]` extras to let consumers install only the converters they need, e.g. `pip install markitdown[pdf]`.

nodes.name: test_invalid_flag
nodes.summary: Spawns the markitdown CLI with an unrecognized '--foobar' flag and asserts it exits with a non-zero status and an appropriate error message.

nodes.name: test_cli_vectors.py
nodes.summary: End-to-end CLI integration tests that drive the markitdown command-line tool against the shared GENERAL_TEST_VECTORS / DATA_URI_TEST_VECTORS fixtures, covering stdin/stdout/file I/O, URL conversion, and data-URI image embedding, by invoking the CLI as a subprocess and asserting on its output.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a dual-import pattern (`if __name__ == '__main__'` vs relative import) so the module can run both as a standalone script and as part of the pytest package, and skips remote-URL tests in CI via the GITHUB_ACTIONS environment variable.

nodes.name: test_output_to_file
nodes.summary: Drives the markitdown CLI to convert each test-vector fixture to an output file and asserts the resulting Markdown contains/excludes the expected strings defined in the shared test vectors.

nodes.name: test_input_from_stdin_without_hints
nodes.summary: Pipes each test-vector fixture into the markitdown CLI via stdin without filename/MIME hints and asserts the produced Markdown still matches the expected must-include/must-not-include strings, validating content-based format sniffing.

nodes.name: test_convert_url
nodes.summary: Tests that the markitdown CLI can fetch and convert a remote URL directly, asserting on the expected content; skipped in CI environments to avoid network flakiness.

nodes.name: test_output_to_file_with_data_uris
nodes.summary: Verifies the CLI's image-embedding option produces Markdown containing base64 data URIs for embedded images, checking output against the DATA_URI_TEST_VECTORS expectations.

nodes.name: test_cu_converter.py
nodes.summary: Large unit test suite for ContentUnderstandingConverter covering file-type/MIME acceptance and restriction logic, file-type detection and canonicalization, smart analyzer-modality routing, mocked end-to-end conversion, registration priority versus DocumentIntelligenceConverter, CLI argument wiring (--use-cu/--cu-*), and missing-dependency error messaging.
nodes.languageNotes: Organizes ~50 test cases into 12 pytest test classes by concern (acceptance, MIME handling, routing, modality resolution, mocked conversion, CLI args, missing deps), and uses unittest.mock.patch/MagicMock extensively to isolate the converter from the real Azure SDK.

nodes.name: _make_converter
nodes.summary: Test helper that constructs a ContentUnderstandingConverter via __new__ (bypassing __init__ and its Azure SDK dependency) and manually sets its analyzer id, modality, and accepted file types for isolated unit testing.

nodes.name: TestAcceptsExtension
nodes.summary: Tests ContentUnderstandingConverter.accepts() logic for recognizing supported file extensions and rejecting unsupported ones.

nodes.name: TestAcceptsMime
nodes.summary: Tests ContentUnderstandingConverter.accepts() logic for recognizing supported MIME types and rejecting unsupported ones.

nodes.name: TestAcceptsFileTypeRestriction
nodes.summary: Tests that ContentUnderstandingConverter correctly restricts acceptance to a configured subset of file types (e.g. PDF-only or audio-only) and that CLI value strings (webm, m4v) map consistently to internal file-type values.

nodes.name: TestDetectFileType
nodes.summary: Tests the internal _detect_file_type, _canonical_mime_type, and _content_type_for helpers, covering detection from MIME alone, alias resolution, conflict resolution between extension and MIME hints, and how restrictions interact with MIME-based detection.

nodes.name: TestSmartRouting
nodes.summary: Tests the smart analyzer-routing behavior that selects between custom analyzers and prebuilt Azure analyzers based on detected modality (document/image/audio/video), including auto-routing fallbacks and MIME-alias handling.

nodes.name: TestResolveAnalyzerModality
nodes.summary: Tests _resolve_analyzer_modality's logic for mapping known prebuilt analyzer IDs (document/audio/video/image) to modalities, falling back to dynamic analyzer lookup for unknown IDs, and surfacing ValueError on lookup failure.

nodes.name: TestGetModality
nodes.summary: Tests the _get_modality helper that classifies file types into document, image, video, and audio modality categories.

nodes.name: TestConvertMock
nodes.summary: Mocks the Azure Content Understanding SDK end-to-end and verifies ContentUnderstandingConverter.convert() returns correct Markdown for PDF, MP4, WAV, and JPEG inputs, plus correct handling of empty analysis results.

nodes.name: TestGetAnalyzerError
nodes.summary: Verifies that requesting a nonexistent custom analyzer raises a ValueError with an informative message.

nodes.name: TestRegistrationPriority
nodes.summary: Asserts that ContentUnderstandingConverter is registered with higher priority than DocumentIntelligenceConverter so it is preferred when both are enabled for overlapping file types.

nodes.name: TestCLIArgs
nodes.summary: Tests the CLI argument wiring for Content Understanding flags (--use-cu, --cu-endpoint, --cu-file-types), including mutual exclusivity with --use-docintel, validation of file-type list parsing, and that parsed args are correctly forwarded as kwargs to MarkItDown.

nodes.name: TestMissingDependency
nodes.summary: Verifies that attempting to use the Content Understanding converter without the optional Azure SDK dependency installed raises a clear, actionable error message.

nodes.name: test_docintel_html.py
nodes.summary: Focused unit test verifying that DocumentIntelligenceConverter correctly accepts HTML input both by file extension ('.html') and by MIME type ('text/html', 'application/xhtml+xml'), constructing the converter via __new__ to bypass Azure SDK initialization.
nodes.languageNotes: Serves as the structural template that test_cu_converter.py explicitly says it follows for bypassing __init__ via __new__ when testing accepts() logic.

nodes.name: _make_converter
nodes.summary: Test helper that constructs a DocumentIntelligenceConverter via __new__, bypassing __init__, and assigns its accepted file types directly for isolated accepts() testing.

nodes.name: equations.docx
nodes.summary: Binary Word document fixture containing mathematical equations (OMML), used to verify that the DOCX converter correctly extracts and renders block/inline equations as Markdown/LaTeX-style `$$...$$` blocks.

nodes.name: random.bin
nodes.summary: Arbitrary binary blob fixture with no recognizable format, used to verify that MarkItDown raises UnsupportedFormatException for unsupported content and to test forced-extension conversion attempts.

nodes.name: rlink.docx
nodes.summary: Binary Word document fixture containing a relative/external link reference (rlink) to an on-disk file, used to test the DOCX converter's handling of external link resolution and `keep_data_uris` behavior.

nodes.name: test_notebook.ipynb
nodes.summary: Sample Jupyter Notebook fixture used by the test vectors and converter dispatch tests to validate that the ipynb converter correctly renders cells, outputs, and metadata to Markdown.

nodes.name: test_outlook_msg.msg
nodes.summary: Binary Outlook MSG email fixture (`application/vnd.ms-outlook`) used by the test vectors to validate the Outlook MSG converter's extraction of headers, body, and metadata into Markdown.

nodes.name: test_with_comment.docx
nodes.summary: Binary Word document fixture containing reviewer comments, used to verify that the DOCX converter extracts and surfaces document comments in the converted Markdown output.

nodes.name: test.docx
nodes.summary: Primary binary Word document fixture used across the general test-vector suite and data-URI tests to validate baseline DOCX-to-Markdown conversion, including content extraction and embedded data URI handling.

nodes.name: test.epub
nodes.summary: Binary EPUB ebook fixture used by the general test vectors to validate the EPUB converter's extraction of chapters, metadata, and text content into Markdown.

nodes.name: test.m4a
nodes.summary: Binary M4A audio fixture used by speech-transcription tests to validate that the audio converter and transcription pipeline correctly produce a textual transcript and metadata in Markdown output.

nodes.name: test.pptx
nodes.summary: Binary PowerPoint presentation fixture used across the general test vectors, exiftool metadata tests, and LLM-captioning tests to validate slide/content extraction and image-description integration.

nodes.name: test.wav
nodes.summary: Binary WAV audio fixture used by the speech-transcription test to validate that the audio converter and transcription backend produce an accurate text transcript in Markdown output.

nodes.name: test.xls
nodes.summary: Binary legacy Excel (.xls) spreadsheet fixture used by the general test vectors to validate the XLS converter's extraction of sheet content into Markdown tables.

nodes.name: test.xlsx
nodes.summary: Binary modern Excel (.xlsx) spreadsheet fixture used by the general test vectors to validate the XLSX converter's extraction of sheet content into Markdown tables.

nodes.name: test_module_misc.py
nodes.summary: Miscellaneous module-level test suite for MarkItDown covering helper APIs and runtime conversion options not exercised by the FileTestVectors -- StreamInfo manipulation, data/file URI handling, DOCX comments and equations, deeply-nested HTML fallback, rlink resolution, exception types, exiftool integration, speech transcription, and optional LLM-based image captioning.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses pytest skip markers driven by environment probes (GITHUB_ACTIONS, OPENAI_API_KEY, exiftool availability) to gracefully disable remote/optional-dependency tests, and also exposes a `__main__` runner that replays the test list sequentially for manual debugging.

nodes.name: test_module_vectors.py
nodes.summary: Parametrized test suite that drives the MarkItDown class through every entry in GENERAL_TEST_VECTORS and DATA_URI_TEST_VECTORS, exercising stream-info guessing, local/stream/HTTP/file/data-URI conversion paths, and `keep_data_uris` behavior across all supported input formats.
nodes.languageNotes: Imports its data fixtures from a sibling `_test_vectors` module using a dual import style (`from _test_vectors import ...` when run directly vs. relative `from ._test_vectors import ...` when run as a package) to support both pytest discovery and `python -m`/direct execution.

nodes.name: test_pdf_masterformat.py
nodes.summary: Focused unit-test module verifying the PDF converter's handling of MasterFormat-style partial section numbering (e.g. '.1', '.2'), asserting the `PARTIAL_NUMBERING_PATTERN` regex behavior and that such fragments are merged rather than incorrectly split during conversion.

nodes.name: test_pdf_tables.py
nodes.summary: Large test module dedicated to validating PDF table-extraction quality -- covering borderless tables, receipts, multipage invoices, academic papers, scanned documents, full-output regression comparisons, Markdown pipe-formatting, and structural row/column consistency, with custom Markdown-table parsing and validation helpers.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines its own lightweight Markdown-table parsing and validation helpers (`extract_markdown_tables`, `validate_table_structure`, `validate_markdown_table`) rather than depending on a Markdown parser library, keeping the test suite self-contained and fast.

nodes.name: test_pdf_memory.py
nodes.summary: Test module validating the PDF converter's memory-optimization behavior -- ensuring `page.close()` is invoked on every processed page, plain-text PDFs fall back to pdfminer, mixed PDFs use per-page form extraction, and memory usage stays roughly constant regardless of page count (via `tracemalloc`-based benchmarks and mocked `pdfplumber`/`pdfminer` backends).
nodes.languageNotes: Heavily mocks `markitdown.converters._pdf_converter.pdfplumber` and `.pdfminer` with `unittest.mock.patch` and synthetic in-memory PDFs (built via `fpdf2` when available) to deterministically test memory behavior without relying on large real-world PDF files.

nodes.name: _make_form_page
nodes.summary: Builds a mock pdfplumber page object that simulates a form-style page (with extractable form fields/widgets) for use in PDF-converter memory and extraction-path tests.

nodes.name: _make_plain_page
nodes.summary: Builds a mock pdfplumber page object that simulates a plain-text page (no extractable form data) so tests can verify the pdfminer fallback path.

nodes.name: _mock_pdfplumber_open
nodes.summary: Returns a mock replacement for `pdfplumber.open` that yields a supplied sequence of mock pages, allowing tests to control exactly what pages the PDF converter iterates over.

nodes.name: _generate_table_pdf
nodes.summary: Generates an in-memory synthetic PDF containing a table (using fpdf2 when available) for use in benchmark tests that measure memory growth across varying page counts.

nodes.name: TestPdfMemoryOptimization
nodes.summary: Test class asserting that the PDF converter closes every page after processing, correctly falls back to pdfminer for plain-text PDFs, and applies per-page form extraction only where appropriate -- using mocked pdfplumber/pdfminer backends and both synthetic and real PDFs.

nodes.name: TestPdfMemoryBenchmark
nodes.summary: Benchmark test class using `tracemalloc` to assert that peak memory usage during PDF conversion does not grow linearly with page count and remains roughly constant across documents of varying sizes.

nodes.name: TestMasterFormatPartialNumbering
nodes.summary: Test class verifying the `PARTIAL_NUMBERING_PATTERN` regex matches MasterFormat-style partial section numbers (e.g. '.1', '.10') but rejects unrelated text, and that the PDF converter merges such fragments with their preceding content rather than splitting them into separate list items.

nodes.name: validate_markdown_table
nodes.summary: Validates that a Markdown table string conforms to expected pipe-delimited formatting and structural conventions (header row, separator row, consistent column counts).

nodes.name: extract_markdown_tables
nodes.summary: Parses a block of converted Markdown text and extracts individual Markdown table substrings for further structural validation by the PDF table-extraction tests.

nodes.name: validate_table_structure
nodes.summary: Checks that an extracted Markdown table has the expected number of rows and columns and that its cell data is internally consistent across all rows.

nodes.name: TestPdfTableExtraction
nodes.summary: Test class asserting that the PDF converter correctly extracts borderless tables, receipts, multipage invoices, academic papers, scanned documents, and movie-theater booking PDFs into well-formed Markdown tables without duplication and with correct positioning.

nodes.name: TestPdfFullOutputComparison
nodes.summary: Test class performing end-to-end comparisons of full converted Markdown output for a variety of PDF document types (movie-theater, sparse borderless tables, multipage repairs, receipts, academic papers, medical scans) against expected content.

nodes.name: TestPdfTableMarkdownFormat
nodes.summary: Test class verifying that PDF-extracted Markdown tables use correct pipe-delimited (`|`) formatting with properly aligned columns and separator rows.

nodes.name: TestPdfTableStructureConsistency
nodes.summary: Test class asserting structural consistency of extracted PDF tables -- verifying row/column counts, that receipts and scanned PDFs correctly produce no spurious tables, and that all extracted table rows maintain consistent cell counts and data integrity.

nodes.name: test_stream_info_operations
nodes.summary: Tests the `StreamInfo` data class's copy-and-update semantics, ensuring fields like mimetype, extension, charset, filename, local path, and URL are correctly merged and overridden when cloning instances.

nodes.name: test_data_uris
nodes.summary: Validates `parse_data_uri` correctly decodes base64 and URL-encoded data URIs into mimetype, attributes, and content for both text and binary payloads.

nodes.name: test_file_uris
nodes.summary: Validates `file_uri_to_path` correctly converts `file://` URIs (including UNC and Windows drive-letter forms) back into local filesystem paths.

nodes.name: test_docx_equations
nodes.summary: Converts the `equations.docx` fixture and asserts that block-level mathematical equations are emitted in the output wrapped in `$$...$$` Markdown/LaTeX delimiters.

nodes.name: test_input_as_strings
nodes.summary: Verifies that MarkItDown can accept raw string/bytes-like input (not just file paths or streams) and convert it correctly, exercising the input-normalization layer.

nodes.name: test_deeply_nested_html_fallback
nodes.summary: Constructs deeply nested HTML structures and asserts that the converter gracefully falls back (rather than raising a `RecursionError`) when parsing exceptionally deep DOM trees.

nodes.name: test_doc_rlink
nodes.summary: Converts the `rlink.docx` fixture with `keep_data_uris=True`, writing a companion file to `/tmp` and asserting that the converter correctly resolves the document's relative external link to the on-disk target content.

nodes.name: test_markitdown_remote
nodes.summary: Skipped-in-CI integration test that exercises MarkItDown's ability to fetch and convert content from a remote URL over the network.

nodes.name: test_speech_transcription
nodes.summary: Converts the `test.wav`, `test.mp3`, and `test.m4a` audio fixtures and asserts that the produced Markdown contains the expected transcript text, validating the speech-to-text pipeline end to end.

nodes.name: test_exceptions
nodes.summary: Asserts that `MarkItDown.convert` raises `UnsupportedFormatException` for unrecognized binary content (`random.bin`) and `FileConversionException` when an unsupported extension is forced for a mismatched file type.

nodes.name: test_markitdown_exiftool
nodes.summary: Verifies that MarkItDown can locate and use the `exiftool` binary -- both via explicit constructor parameter and via the `EXIFTOOL_PATH` environment variable -- to extract image/media metadata during conversion.

nodes.name: test_markitdown_llm_parameters
nodes.summary: Validates that MarkItDown correctly forwards LLM client, model, and prompt parameters into the image-captioning pipeline when converting PPTX/image content, asserting the right calls reach the underlying client.

nodes.name: test_markitdown_llm
nodes.summary: Skipped-without-API-key integration test that exercises real LLM-backed image description generation against the `test.pptx` fixture using the OpenAI client.

nodes.name: test_guess_stream_info
nodes.summary: Parametrized test asserting that `MarkItDown.convert` (and its internal stream-info guessing) correctly infers mimetype, extension, and charset for each file in `GENERAL_TEST_VECTORS`.

nodes.name: test_convert_local
nodes.summary: Parametrized test verifying that converting each test vector from a local filesystem path produces the expected Markdown content.

nodes.name: test_convert_stream_with_hints
nodes.summary: Parametrized test verifying that converting each test vector from an open binary stream, supplemented with explicit `StreamInfo` hints (mimetype/extension), produces correct Markdown output.

nodes.name: test_convert_stream_without_hints
nodes.summary: Parametrized test verifying that converting each test vector from a raw binary stream without any format hints still produces correct Markdown via automatic format sniffing.

nodes.name: test_convert_http_uri
nodes.summary: Skipped-in-CI parametrized test verifying that MarkItDown can fetch and convert each test vector from its remote HTTP(S) URL counterpart.

nodes.name: test_convert_file_uri
nodes.summary: Parametrized test verifying that MarkItDown correctly converts each test vector when addressed via a `file://` URI rather than a plain filesystem path.

nodes.name: test_convert_data_uri
nodes.summary: Parametrized test verifying that MarkItDown correctly converts content supplied as a base64-encoded `data:` URI, asserting the decoded payload is processed identically to a file-based input.

nodes.name: test_convert_keep_data_uris
nodes.summary: Parametrized test (over `DATA_URI_TEST_VECTORS`) asserting that converting a local file with `keep_data_uris=True` preserves embedded resources as inline base64 data URIs in the Markdown output.

nodes.name: test_convert_stream_keep_data_uris
nodes.summary: Parametrized test (over `DATA_URI_TEST_VECTORS`) asserting that converting a binary stream with `keep_data_uris=True` preserves embedded resources as inline base64 data URIs, mirroring the local-file variant.

layers.name: Core Conversion Engine
layers.description: The MarkItDown orchestrator (_markitdown.py), base converter contracts, stream-info data model, exception hierarchy, URI helpers, and CLI/package entry points that drive type detection and dispatch to format converters.

layers.name: Format Converters & Conversion Utilities
layers.description: The full library of format-specific DocumentConverter implementations (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, HTML, audio, image, EPUB, ZIP, YouTube, RSS, Outlook MSG, etc.) plus shared conversion helpers like _CustomMarkdownify, exiftool/transcription utilities, and the DOCX OMML/LaTeX math preprocessing utilities.

layers.name: OCR Plugin (markitdown-ocr)
layers.description: A self-contained plugin package that extends the core PDF/DOCX/PPTX/XLSX converters with LLM-vision-based OCR via a dedicated OCRResult/LLMVisionOCRService layer and a registration module that overrides the built-in converters at higher priority.

layers.name: MCP Server & Sample Plugin
layers.description: Two thin integration packages that wrap the core library for external consumption: the markitdown-mcp Model Context Protocol server (FastMCP/Starlette tool exposing conversion to LLM agents) and the markitdown-sample-plugin reference implementation demonstrating the plugin registration API.

layers.name: Test Suites & Fixtures
layers.description: Pytest suites and binary/text fixtures (DOCX, PDF, XLSX, PPTX, audio, HTML, expected-output Markdown snapshots, test vectors) for all four packages, validating converters, the CLI, the OCR plugin, the MCP server, and the sample plugin end to end.

layers.name: Documentation & Governance
layers.description: Project-level and per-package README files, third-party license attributions, and Microsoft OSS governance documents (SECURITY, SUPPORT, CODE_OF_CONDUCT) that explain installation, usage, plugin development, and community policy.

layers.name: Infrastructure & CI/CD
layers.description: Docker build definitions for the CLI and MCP server images, the dev-container setup, GitHub Actions workflows (pre-commit linting and the matrix test pipeline), Dependabot scheduling, pre-commit hook registration, and repository-level git/tooling attribute files.

layers.name: Package Configuration & Manifests
layers.description: Hatchling-based pyproject.toml manifests for each of the four sub-packages, declaring dependencies, optional extras, console-script entry points, and plugin registration metadata that defines how the monorepo is built and distributed.

tour.title: What MarkItDown Does
tour.description: Start with the project README to understand MarkItDown's purpose: a lightweight Python utility that converts PDFs, Office documents, images, audio, HTML, ZIP archives, YouTube URLs, EPubs and more into Markdown for LLM and text-analysis pipelines. It explains installation (pip, source, Docker), the CLI and Python API, the optional-dependency extras system, the plugin mechanism, Azure integrations, and an important security warning: MarkItDown performs I/O with the privileges of the current process, so untrusted input must be sanitized. This sets up everything the rest of the tour will demonstrate in code.

tour.title: The CLI Entry Point
tour.description: `__main__.py` is where a `markitdown` invocation actually begins -- it parses command-line arguments (input/output files, format hints, plugin toggles, Azure Document Intelligence/Content Understanding options), constructs a `MarkItDown` instance, and writes the resulting Markdown to stdout or a file. As the BFS traversal from this entry point shows, it is the root from which the whole core engine unfolds: it imports `_markitdown.py` directly, making it the natural first code stop after the README's conceptual overview.
tour.languageLesson: Python's `if __name__ == "__main__":` convention combined with a `__main__.py` module lets a package be both importable as a library and runnable directly via `python -m markitdown` or a console-script entry point declared in pyproject.toml.

tour.title: The MarkItDown Orchestrator
tour.description: `_markitdown.py` defines the `MarkItDown` class -- the heart of the project. It registers built-in and plugin converters, guesses file types using MIME hints, extensions, and the `magika` library, and then dispatches local files, in-memory streams, URLs, `data:`/`file:` URIs, and HTTP responses to whichever `DocumentConverter` claims to handle that content. Every conversion path described in the README -- `convert()`, `convert_local()`, `convert_stream()` -- routes through this single orchestrator, making it the most central file in the whole codebase.

tour.title: The Conversion Contract
tour.description: Two small files define the vocabulary every converter shares. `_base_converter.py` declares the abstract `DocumentConverter` class with its `accepts()`/`convert()` contract (plus `DocumentConverterResult`), which every format-specific converter implements -- it has the highest fan-in of any file in the project, meaning nearly everything depends on it. `_stream_info.py` defines the immutable `StreamInfo` dataclass that carries metadata (mimetype, extension, charset, filename, path, url) from the orchestrator's type-guessing logic in Step 3 to whichever converter handles the stream; it has the single highest fan-in count of all 139 nodes, underscoring how central this metadata object is to the dispatch process.
tour.languageLesson: Python's `abc` module and `@dataclass` decorator are used here to formalize a contract: an abstract base class defines the methods every subclass must implement, while a frozen dataclass provides a lightweight, immutable value object passed between layers -- patterns equivalent to interfaces and value types in statically typed languages.

tour.title: The Converters Package Barrel
tour.description: `converters/__init__.py` is the package's busiest export hub -- it imports and re-exports every concrete converter class (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, HTML, audio, image, YouTube, ZIP, EPUB, and more) so `MarkItDown` (Step 3) can register them all in one place. With the highest fan-out of any node in the project, this file is your map of 'every format MarkItDown understands' -- exactly the list the README enumerates, now visible as a concrete Python package structure.
tour.languageLesson: Python 'barrel' modules (`__init__.py` files that re-export from sibling modules) create a clean public surface: callers write `from markitdown.converters import PdfConverter` instead of reaching into internal submodules, while still allowing each converter to live in its own focused file.

tour.title: Document Converters in Action
tour.description: With the contract from Step 4 in hand, look at three representative converters that show how diverse the implementations can be. `_pdf_converter.py` uses pdfminer to reconstruct numbered lists, render word-level layouts as Markdown tables, and detect form fields -- the most complex single converter by fan-out. `_docx_converter.py` pre-processes embedded math equations (delegating to the OMML/LaTeX utilities you'll see later) and converts the document to HTML via the optional `mammoth` dependency. `_html_converter.py` and its companion `_markdownify.py` (a customized `MarkdownConverter` subclass) form the shared HTML-to-Markdown rendering engine that DOCX, XLSX, EPUB, RSS, Wikipedia, and Bing-SERP converters all delegate to underneath -- explaining why `_html_converter.py` ranks among the most depended-upon files in the project.
tour.languageLesson: Notice the 'delegate to a shared renderer' pattern: rather than each format reimplementing Markdown rendering, DOCX/XLSX/EPUB/RSS converters all funnel their HTML output through one `_CustomMarkdownify` instance -- a form of composition that keeps formatting rules (heading style, link sanitization, image truncation) consistent and centrally maintainable.

tour.title: Shared Conversion Utilities
tour.description: Beyond the converters themselves, a layer of shared helpers does the heavy lifting that multiple formats need. `_exiftool.py` shells out to the `exiftool` binary (with a version check guarding against CVE-2021-22204) to extract metadata from images and audio. `_transcribe_audio.py` and `_llm_caption.py` provide speech-to-text and image-captioning helpers used by the audio and image converters. The `converter_utils/docx/math` package parses Office Math Markup Language (OMML) and converts it to LaTeX via lookup tables in `latex_dict.py` -- the deepest dependency chain in the codebase (BFS depth 5-6), reached only when a DOCX contains mathematical equations.

tour.title: Extending MarkItDown -- The Plugin API
tour.description: MarkItDown's converter list isn't fixed -- the README describes a plugin system, and `markitdown-sample-plugin` is the reference implementation showing exactly how it works. `_plugin.py` declares a `__plugin_interface_version__`, implements a minimal `RtfConverter` (built on the same `DocumentConverter` contract from Step 4) using the `striprtf` library, and exposes a `register_converters` hook. The package is wired up entirely through a `markitdown.plugin` entry point in its `pyproject.toml`, which `MarkItDown` auto-discovers at runtime when plugins are enabled -- turning a third-party package into a first-class converter without touching the core library's code.
tour.languageLesson: Python's `entry_points` mechanism (declared in `pyproject.toml` under a custom group like `markitdown.plugin`) lets a library discover externally-installed packages at runtime via `importlib.metadata` -- the same plumbing that powers pytest plugins, Flask extensions, and Click command groups.

tour.title: A Production Plugin -- markitdown-ocr
tour.description: Building on the plugin pattern from Step 8, `markitdown-ocr` is a much larger, self-contained plugin package that adds LLM-vision-based OCR to PDF, DOCX, PPTX, and XLSX conversion. `_ocr_service.py` defines the shared `OCRResult` dataclass and `LLMVisionOCRService` (which sends base64-encoded images to an OpenAI-compatible vision model), while `_plugin.py` wires that service into four OCR-enhanced converter subclasses and registers them with MarkItDown at a *higher priority* than the built-ins -- effectively replacing them when the plugin is active. This demonstrates the plugin system's full power: not just adding new formats, but overriding existing ones.
tour.languageLesson: Priority-based registration is a common extensibility pattern: converters declare a numeric priority, and the dispatcher tries higher-priority candidates first. This lets a plugin 'shadow' a built-in implementation cleanly, without monkey-patching or forking the original code.

tour.title: MarkItDown as an MCP Server
tour.description: The `markitdown-mcp` package wraps the core library as a Model Context Protocol server, letting LLM agents call file-to-Markdown conversion as a tool. Its `__main__.py` registers a `convert_to_markdown` tool via FastMCP, builds a Starlette ASGI app supporting both SSE and Streamable-HTTP transports, and parses CLI flags to launch over STDIO or HTTP. The package README documents Docker deployment, Claude Desktop integration, and -- importantly -- a security warning about running the server without authentication, echoing the same 'sanitize untrusted input' theme from the project README in Step 1.

tour.title: Test Vectors -- One Source of Truth
tour.description: `_test_vectors.py` defines `FileTestVector`, a dataclass describing, per fixture file, exactly which strings must (and must not) appear in the converted Markdown. `GENERAL_TEST_VECTORS` and `DATA_URI_TEST_VECTORS` are then consumed by both the library-level (`test_module_vectors.py`) and CLI-level (`test_cli_vectors.py`) suites, ensuring the CLI from Step 2 and the `MarkItDown` API from Step 3 are tested against the same expectations. This 'single source of truth' design means adding a new format only requires adding fixtures and vectors, not duplicating assertions across two test layers.
tour.languageLesson: Data-driven testing with `@dataclass` fixtures and `pytest.mark.parametrize` separates 'what to test' (a declarative list of expectations) from 'how to test it' (the runner logic), making it cheap to extend coverage to new file formats.

tour.title: Containerizing MarkItDown
tour.description: Two Dockerfiles show how the project ships its two faces. The root `Dockerfile` builds the CLI image on `python:3.13-slim-bullseye`, installs `ffmpeg` and `exiftool` runtime dependencies (the same tools `_exiftool.py` and `_transcribe_audio.py` from Step 7 shell out to), pip-installs `markitdown[all]` plus the sample plugin, and runs as a non-root user with `markitdown` as the entrypoint. `packages/markitdown-mcp/Dockerfile` does the analogous build for the MCP server from Step 10, additionally setting `MARKITDOWN_ENABLE_PLUGINS=True` so the OCR and sample plugins from Steps 8-9 can be auto-discovered inside the container.
tour.languageLesson: Layer ordering matters for Docker build-cache efficiency: install OS packages and pin Python versions before copying application code, since rarely-changing layers should sit beneath frequently-changing ones. Running as a non-root user is a security best practice that limits the blast radius if the containerized process is compromised -- especially relevant given this project's own warning about I/O privileges.

tour.title: CI -- Tests and Linting on Every PR
tour.description: Two GitHub Actions workflows are the project's quality gates, run on every pull request. `tests.yml` sets up Python 3.10/3.11/3.12 in a matrix, installs Hatch, and runs `hatch test` inside `packages/markitdown` -- exercising the test-vector suite from Step 11 across all supported interpreters. `pre-commit.yml` installs and runs `pre-commit run --all-files`, which (per `.pre-commit-config.yaml`) enforces the `black` formatter pinned to a specific revision. Together they enforce that every change both behaves correctly and looks consistent before it can be merged.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions matrix builds (`strategy.matrix`) run the same job across multiple interpreter versions in parallel, catching version-specific regressions early. Pinning tool revisions (e.g., `black==23.7.0`) in pre-commit config keeps formatting deterministic across contributors and CI runs alike.

tour.title: Packaging the Monorepo
tour.description: Step back and see how the four sub-packages you've toured -- `markitdown`, `markitdown-mcp`, `markitdown-ocr`, and `markitdown-sample-plugin` -- are each independently versioned and distributed. Every `pyproject.toml` uses Hatchling as its build backend, declares its dependencies and optional extras (the README's installation matrix made concrete), and registers either a console-script entry point (`markitdown`, `markitdown-mcp`) or a `markitdown.plugin` entry point (for the OCR and sample plugins, as you saw in Steps 8-9). This is the blueprint that ties the whole monorepo's independent pieces back into the single coherent project the README describes.
tour.languageLesson: Hatchling's `pyproject.toml`-driven configuration replaces legacy `setup.py` boilerplate: dependencies, optional 'extras' groups (e.g., `pip install markitdown[pdf,docx]`), console scripts, and plugin entry points are all declared as data, making the package's public contract easy to audit at a glance.
